SEARCH
Package org.javalabs.jpa.dialect

Interface SQLDialect

All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractDBDialect, DB2Dialect, DerbyDialect, H2Dialect, MySqlDialect, OracleDialect, PostgresDialect, SybaseDialect
public interface SQLDialect
The SQL Dialect.

The constantly evolving nature of the SQL standard has given rise to a number of SQL dialects among the various vendors and platforms. These dialects commonly evolve because a given database vendor’s user community requires capabilities in the database before the ANSI committee creates an applicable standard. Because ANSI had not yet developed a standard for these important features at the time users began to demand them, RDBMS developers and vendors created their own commands and syntax. In fact, some of the earliest vendors from the 1980s have variances in the most elementary commands, such as SELECT, because their implementations predate the standards. ANSI is now refining standards that address these inconsistencies.

The jpa-lite SQLDialect aims at providing consistencies across different RDBMSs, when it comes to the following operation:

  • Create Table
  • Drop Table
  • Create Constraint
  • Drop Constraint
  • Create Index
  • Drop Index
Author:
Sudiptasish Chanda

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description

  • Method Details

    • db_url

      String db_url(String host, String port, String db)
      Form and return the jdbc database url.
      Parameters:
      host - Remote database host name
      port - Remote database port
      db - Remote database name.
      Returns:
      String The connection string compatible with underlying database.

    • run_command

      String run_command(String sqlFile)
      Return the database specific run command.
      Parameters:
      sqlFile - A .sql file to be run
      Returns:
      String The status of the run

    • query_tables

      String query_tables(String schema)
      Return the sql query to fetch the table names under a specific schema.
      Parameters:
      schema - The schema name the tables belong to.
      Returns:
      String Return the well formatted db specific sql query string.

    • create_table

      String create_table(ClassDescriptor desc)
      Return the database specific CREATE TABLE syntax.
      Parameters:
      desc - The class descriptor.
      Returns:
      String Return the create table script for the jpa entity represented by this class descriptor.

    • drop_table

      String drop_table(ClassDescriptor desc)
      Return the database specific DROP TABLE syntax.
      Parameters:
      desc - The class descriptor
      Returns:
      String The well formatted drop table script.

    • primary_key

      String primary_key(ClassDescriptor desc)
      Return the database specific ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT syntax.
      Parameters:
      desc - The class descriptor
      Returns:
      String The well formatted sql script to create the primary key for the table.

    • index

      String index(ClassDescriptor desc)
      Return the database specific CREATE {UNIQUE} INDEX syntax.
      Parameters:
      desc - The class descriptor
      Returns:
      String The well formatted sql script to create the unique index for the table.

    • foreign_key

      String foreign_key(ClassDescriptor desc)
      Return the database specific foreign key statement.
      Parameters:
      desc - The class descriptor
      Returns:
      String The well formatted sql script to create the foreign key constraint for the table.

    • data_type

      String data_type(Class<?> javatype)
      Return the rdbms specific data type for a given java data type.

      Different RDBMS vendors have their own way of defining the data types. E.g., a string column in Oracle is defined as CHAR or VARCHAR2, whereas the same in Postgres is defined as VARCHAR. Therefore, depending on the current dialect, this API will return the appropriate data type.

      Parameters:
      javatype - The java data type.
      Returns:
      String The corresponding rdbms data type.

    • timestamp

      String timestamp()
      Return the timestamp query for this database.
      Returns:
      String Return the system timestamp of the database server.

    • version

      String version()
      Return the version query for this database.
      Returns:
      String Return the version of the database

    • metadataClasses

      List<Class<?>> metadataClasses()
      Return the classes needed to query the db and table metadata. These classes are internal to vendor, and are jpa entities.
      Returns:
      List Return the metadata class list.

    • db_table_metadata

      String db_table_metadata(jakarta.persistence.EntityManager em, Map<String,Object> props)
      Query the database to get the metadata for table and associated columns and constraints. It will generate the result in the form of a orm.xml.
      Parameters:
      em -
      props -
      Returns:
      String
